## About The Pull Request
Uses do_after to help a miner auto dig a tile based on toolspeed (I
think this is the same as the cooldown for its attack speed?)
I'm not entirely sure how to get the do_after to disable once you are
stunned from being out of stamina but it only tries to fire but fails.
It causes a double panting emote which isn't the worst thing in the
world but if anyone has an idea on how to fix that I could do it.

You still manually hit rocks, if you think I should add it to them I
could.

Makes Lupian Tolerated instead of Respected (#2923)

## About The Pull Request

Makes Lupians tolerated instead of respected, this does nothing in game,
but should we want to limit certain roles like duke, knight, man at
arms, etc to respected races only, this would limit them being able to
play those roles.

## Why It's Good For The Game

Lupians have their own kingdoms, their own land, and an entirely
different social structure to humen leading them to give preferential
treatment to their own packmates while fighting with those of rival
packs or those of disputed heritage (mutts).

They fight amongst themselves and don't really respect those in power
unless it can be enforced, they wouldn't be respected and IMO they
should not be seen as the duke of rockhill and any lupian nobleman
should be seen as inherently cunning.

## Proof of Testing (Required)

N/A

Jam goblin encampment revamp (#2888)

<!-- Write **BELOW** The Headers and **ABOVE** The comments else it may
not be viewable. -->

## About The Pull Request
I rearranged the Goblin Encampment with a similar eye towards
player-traffic. Instead of a long, thin snaking corridoor, all things
connect to a large central common room with the throne and dining
tables, with the most commonly used things most immediately to hand by
the entrance (armoury, beds) and the more niche things (mine, kitchen,
storage) tucked away on the edge.

Spent a bit of time rearranging the goblin encampment. A big thing is
changing the overall layout, rather than being tucked away behind a
door, the throne-room and dining hall are one big central chamber that
all the other rooms snake off of. This makes it more of a common meeting
place because you'll be going through there to get to the other stuff,
rather than just going through the plain corridor between them.

The storage room and mine are niche rooms and are moved further away
from the entrance onto the western side. It has a little rat surprise
for dilligent miners. They're still accessible, but you aren't reminded
of how empty they are every time you leave the base. The kitchen is
tucked away closer to the dining hall, and connected directly to the
"outside" area (which is exclusively used for farming from what I
understand). I made the kitchen messier and made the 'outside' a bit
less depressing.

The chieftain's room is behind the chieftain's throne, and the
chieftain's hoard is behind the chieftain's room. I think it just makes
sense. Means the chieftain doesn't have to waste so much time going back
and forth, and it reflects a bit of personality, the chieftain is
self-centered and greedy, and keeps their treasures close. Mine mine
mine!.
To reflect a recent buff giving whip skill to goblin chieftain, the
chieftain also starts with a whip in their drawer. Since goblins can
definitely speak imperial now, there's no serious need to keep
talkstones around, taking a neck slot and having to throw one at your
interlocutor and hope they put it on and kindly give it back when you're
done talking. So instead, Cursed Collars! A much more fitting bit of
neck-based magical equipment for goblins to have.

The Shaman's room is also accessible from the little throne entrance and
from the prison area. The prison area has (another) rat-based surprise,
which can be eaten or kept as a pet or used to feed unlucky prisoners
to. It also has a NEW room, a combination surgery-and-torture-chamber
with some basic stone improvised surgical tools.

Lastly, instead of a steel dagger and three hunting knives, they start
with two iron daggers and two hunting knives. If you want steel, you'd
better steal it from someone. There's also a spare axe for goblin
Raiders and spare bows and arrows for rabble/boltslingers. Also,
importantly, they have a couple spare Maces for the brutes and for the
chieftain if he wants one.

Now, instead of having that really awkward room with a wandering saiga
in it that continuously blocks your way into the armoury, I was instead
going to include a little Stable room - but I thought it'd be funnier,
in that goblin way, to just have it kinda be chilling in the central
chamber. Up to the player goblins whether they kill and eat it or keep
it as a pet or for war. And with more space to roam, it's less likely to
actually be a problem.

Lastly, the area is generally more well decorated, with messes and
splats and stains and rats and poo littering the floor to make it feel
more lived in and goblin-y

## Why It's Good For The Game

The original encampment was drab and lonely and awkwardly laid out.
Goblins never went to the throne room because it was so out-of-the-way
and inconvenient, and making a quick stop to eat, sleep and restock on
gear had a lot of added tedium by the long, snaking corridor separating
everything. It should be a more fun area for a full warband to base
their operation in and get rowdy and drink together, and more convenient
and less lonely for solo goblins to use as a den between mischief.

Renames Accents - Adds Three (#2831)

<!-- Write **BELOW** The Headers and **ABOVE** The comments else it may
not be viewable. -->

## About The Pull Request

Renames current accent name scheme and adds two new ones.

## Why It's Good For The Game

It isn't. Just adds more useless accents

Eventually when there is a good majority of accents, they will be
renamed from their racial identity and more culture in nature. Each race
will have their own unique accent that other races will be unable to
grab. However the other accents will still be available.

Currently we can not do this, without limiting the accents to so few. So
until we have enough of a "pool" for accents, anyone is able to pick the
racial specific accents at this time.

>New Elven Accent (East Elven)
Just phonetics and some minor word replacements, based off of "korean"
language with some slang. Tried to decrease the amount of word
replacements. Syllables and such make it more 'fun' and harder to read.

>Original Elven Accent renamed to West Elven accent
Added a couple syllables and renamed.

>Original Elven Accent copypasted to create "Otavian" accent
Just to represent an unknown region that represents the slavic area. In
the future, we will add some small word replacements and specific
syllables that are from specific regions to make it some more distinct
than the "west elven."

>New Gronn Accent
Pulls half orc word replacements and adds some syllables and such from a
mongolian accent to make it more incromphehensible. Until Gronn have
proper lore, this is fine for now. But in the event the Gronn are
properly representated outside of being just half-orcs, I will most
likely purge the middlespeak from them and provide proper speech.

>New Lupian Accent
Based from Polish. From this specific #2864 PR.

This will require test-merging and must not be immediately merged. This
is because I do not know English very well, and I will need live-testing
of the accent, particularly the 'elven' one as I do not know how the
playerbase will interpret it much less use it in general.

Gronn accent is just half-orc word replacement with new syllables,
starts, and ends.

Fix for remaining cases of swift undead (#2931)

<!-- Write **BELOW** The Headers and **ABOVE** The comments else it may
not be viewable. -->

## About The Pull Request
This PR fixes the remaining cases of undead with infinite stamina being
able to use Swift intent. Due to an oversight, skeletons revived by
necromancers and liches could still have Swift intent available in
certain circumstances, which should now be impossible due to moving the
swift-prevention logic into the turn_to_minion proc, which should catch
any existing case that isn't a natural skeleton spawn (which is handled
separately), while also preventing the issue from reoccurring in future
uses of skeletons (provided they use the turn_to_minion proc).

<!-- Describe The Pull Request. Please be sure every change is
documented or this can delay review and even discourage maintainers from
merging your PR! -->

## Why It's Good For The Game
Again, due to Swift being balanced by heavy stamina consumption, its use
on (most) undead, which have unlimited stamina, is supposed to be
disabled to prevent undead having too unfair of an advantage over the
living.

<!-- Argue for the merits of your changes and how they benefit the game,
especially if they are controversial and/or far reaching. If you can't
actually explain WHY what you are doing will improve the game, then it
probably isn't good for the game in the first place. -->

testing included four cases: entering the body both by dying and being
raised without leaving it, and by accepting the prompt from the lobby;
and being raised via Raise Undead and via Raise Lesser Undead. In all
tested cases, the minions lacked Swift intent.

Fix for Death Knights who spawn via prompt not being properly initialised (#2933)

<!-- Write **BELOW** The Headers and **ABOVE** The comments else it may
not be viewable. -->

## About The Pull Request
This PR fixes a longstanding issue with Death Knight spawning -
specifically, that Death Knights who spawn via the prompt, rather than
through the latejoin interface, spawn in a bugged state. These Death
knights would have no spells or skills, a generic name, and would be
alive rather than undead.

This issue was caused by a bad call to equipOutfit, which was passed, as
an argument, the Death Knight job, rather than the outfit. The proper
approach is to call SSJob.EquipRank, passing in the Death Knight, the
_title_ of the job, and an indication that the mob is latejoining. (This
boolean is essential to prevent a runtime error from attempting to
access new player information which is not guaranteed to exist.)

The forced movement of the mob to the vampyre lord has been moved after
the EquipRank call, to prevent the latejoining Death Knight from
appearing at one of the standard latejoin locations.

<!-- Describe The Pull Request. Please be sure every change is
documented or this can delay review and even discourage maintainers from
merging your PR! -->

## Why It's Good For The Game
Prior to this PR, Death Knights spawning from the prompt would appear in
a bugged state, with no class stats, spells, skills, or traits, and
would spawn living rather than dead. This meant that less scrupulous
vampyre lords might turn them into spawn; more scrupulous ones might
instead put them toward manual labour or simply kill them, neither of
which are very interesting for the person who opts to become one. This
was not an issue with Death Knights who joined via the latejoin
interface, which is possible if no players responded to the prompt.

With the change, Death Knights who join via the prompt should function
correctly, rather than providing either an unlimited pool from which to
drink and create spawn. Death Knights are still limited in number to
three - a limit which may actually be reached now that they will
reliably add themselves to the list of Death Knights, since bugged Death
Knights did not. It's unclear to me whether this fix will prove to be a
buff to vampyres or not; I suppose time will tell, but after this
change, as with many of my fixes that touch elements of balance, we will
at least be able to tweak Death Knights with the knowledge that they
spawn as intended.

<!-- Argue for the merits of your changes and how they benefit the game,
especially if they are controversial and/or far reaching. If you can't
actually explain WHY what you are doing will improve the game, then it
probably isn't good for the game in the first place. -->


## Proof of Testing (Required)
A screenshot from a friend who aided me in testing, having spawned as a
Death Knight via the prompt. Note the presence of spells, Death Knight
equipment, undead eyes, lack of pain, and skeletonisation. Previously,
this would have simply been a human with some silly name like "Justice
Beach" (yes, this is an actual name that appeared as I was figuring out
this issue).

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3d9d0762-b76e-45e1-a1dd-e390a4d2715f)

Added ability to recolour character chatlog text on a per-recipient basis (#2921)

<!-- Write **BELOW** The Headers and **ABOVE** The comments else it may
not be viewable. -->

## About The Pull Request
This PR adds the ability to define chat colours to be perceived by a
given player when a different given player speaks. To offer an example,
if person A feeds person B a love potion, person B's text in the chat
box will appear in pink to indicate the supernatural influence they have
over person A.

This is achieved through a new list stored in each Mind of
"special_people". This list takes player character names as keys and
strings representing colour hash codes as values; when the owning Mind
receives a message, if the player is on their list, the associated
colour will be used when printing their text to the chat log.

This ability is currently applied in four scenarios:
- When a person is fed a love potion by another person (pink);
- When a person is a Vampyre Spawn, seeing the text of the Vampyre Lord
(red);
- When a person is a Death Knight, seeing the text of the Vampyre Lord
(red);
- When a person is a raised undead, seeing the text of the necromancer
or lich that raised them (purple).

<!-- Describe The Pull Request. Please be sure every change is
documented or this can delay review and even discourage maintainers from
merging your PR! -->

## Why It's Good For The Game
At present, there is some amount of communication of the level of
influence the dominant party has in these relationships - typically in
the form of objective text or a chat message received at the moment that
the influence is first applied. However, this influence isn't really
communicated on an ongoing basis, which I think can lead to some - not
all - players underestimating it. I think applying special text colours
in cases where some manner of mental influence is especially profound,
with an appropriate colour to the type of influence, could go some way
towards remedying that situation.

There's an additional practical layer to this - in cases where, say, the
vampyre lord or lich has become powerful and has a lot of minions, there
might be a lot of chatter, making it hard to pick out the leader's
comments; this feature will make it _much_ easier for Vampyres to hear
the vampyre lord (at least in person - not, for the time being,
telepathically), and likewise for skeletons hearing their masters.

<!-- Argue for the merits of your changes and how they benefit the game,
especially if they are controversial and/or far reaching. If you can't
actually explain WHY what you are doing will improve the game, then it
probably isn't good for the game in the first place. -->

in-game formatting, including markdown, whispers, shouting, yelling, and
combined emotes and dialogue. Note that green is currently unused by any
implementation of this functionality - this was added via variable
editing for testing purposes.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dd1b5e31-f49f-4d92-b842-1da6d85abcec)

Image showing a friend, Skyglazer, receiving pink text after being fed a
love potion. (I can confirm I was seeing plain-coloured, rather than
pink, text). Pardon the contents of the actual text here; I'd previously
missed the hash from the code in the previous round of testing.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f2b70f2d-dd3e-42d1-a82e-072cb7ff78aa)

Image showing Skyglazer receiving red text as a Vampyre Spawn, when
speaking with the Vampyre Lord.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0818419e-c569-4856-a519-f548fc1ebe6c)

Image showing Skyglazer receiving purple text as a skeleton, having been
raised by the Lich. (Again, pardon the text; we determined an ideal
colour as we were testing, but the initial colour was successfully
applied.)

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6375dbd9-2101-4d07-a389-3c7437de1d47)

And Skyglazer confirming, after dying, returning to the lobby, and being
given control of a new body, that these text colours were no longer

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6dcd2187-3dbf-4cef-b329-c605795aaa1e)

We were also able to confirm that the colours persist through a lich
dying and resurrecting, due to using the same real_name.

NPC Mobility Update (#2937)

## About The Pull Request
Fixes NPCs doing nothing when they have a grab in their offhand.
Gives non-deadite NPCs a 5% chance to attempt to leap to close the
distance between them and their target (60% for goblins).
Gives non-deadite NPCs the ability to jump over lava/openturfs to get to
their target.
Gives NPCs a reaction time based on SPD/PER to make fighting a little
easier.
Makes quick enemies 'juke' their targets by backpedaling after hitting.
*Adds a surprise feature that will help us later.*
Fixes some misc. issues.
Removes some commented-out code that shouldn't have passed code review.

## Why It's Good For The Game
Better NPCs. Scarier NPCs. I want to make you cry.

Farming overhaul (Skills affect farming), Mining overhaul (mines now seperated ore types), Map changes (#2739)

<!-- Write **BELOW** The Headers and **ABOVE** The comments else it may
not be viewable. -->

## About The Pull Request
This PR Overhauls farming. Reorganizes the mining map, and tweaks the
vanguard fort; as well as relocates the soilsons and butcher closer to
town.
### Farming Changes: 
The majority of the world now has 'weak' dirt. When tilled, it becomes
'poor' soil with a bad quality var for farming. Similarly, there is now
'great' dirt, which becomes great soil, and regular current dirt that
becomes current soil.
Soil quality directly affects chance to get extra harvest, chance to
ruin crops, and chance to ruin crops a little.
The three soil types also have differant maximums for water, and
nutrition. Poor soil holds half the water and nutrition of standard,
standard soil is the(current), Feracious soil has 1.5 times the maximum
amount of water and nutrition than normal soil.

By default, tilling grass with grant poor dirt. Soilsons start with
great dirt in their farming areas, as well as great soil in a few spots.
The Towner farmer homes have 'average' soil within their fenced area, as
does the physician.
If grass is ploughed with a plough (large dragging object) It will
instead yield standard dirt.

Standard dirt and great dirt will be craftable with level 3 farming
required for standard soil, and level 5 farming required for great soil.

Seeds can no longer be used to create 'soil' plots by themselves at 0
farming skill. This can be done at farming skill 2, however.

Bashing open produce/plants for seeds is now skill based instead of
random; with a significant bonus provided for using a thresher to get
seeds.


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
### Mining Changes:
A second mine has been created to the east in the cave system near the
water lake.
The former western mine has been moved a bit more west, and now features
a levelbelow. The West mine now specifically has copper and tin, and no
iron and coal.
Similarly the new Eastern mine has iron and coal, but no copper and tin.
In the same manner, the lavafissure-bog mine has recieved a significant
boost in that it functions like the eastern mine but has higher drop
rates for valuable ore, as well as more likely to hold ore in the first
place.
Cinnabar now only comes from the lava fissure in the bog.

Bandits can no longer get ore from mining.


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
### Map Tweaks and Changes:
Edit 5/27: This PR has been merged with a seperate map PR moving around
things in lowtown and approved by a maptainer. I'm not 100% sure on all
the changes involved in it, though it primarily tweaks the mine to the
west and adjusts the vanguard fort.

The soilsons has been relocated in between the lowtown and hightown.
Hopefully this will provide a bit more foottraffic, leaving them less
isolated in the middle of nowhere.
The primary mine has been moved more westward (filled primarily with
copper and tin)
A new eastern mine has been made filled primarily with iron and coal.
Dirt everywhere on the map has been replaced with 'poor' dirt.
The vanguard fort has been made more secure; Windows in the dungeon
facing outside are gone, the southern rooftop now has a murderhole wall;
the northern roof with training area now has palisades.
Re-adds wolves to gem cave.
<!-- Describe The Pull Request. Please be sure every change is
documented or this can delay review and even discourage maintainers from
merging your PR! -->

## Why It's Good For The Game
This should hopefully reduce the ease at which people who aren't farmers
are farming things and flooding the in game economy with food; while
making the soilsons stand out compared to druids and towner farmers.

The changes to mining should hopefully be a QOL matter for miners so
they can actually /pick/ what sort of resources they're trying to get
instead of having to hope they get the correct one. For that matter,
since we buffed the lava mine in the bog to make it more valuable,
bandits can no longer get ore from mining- something that probably
should've been done ages ago.


The vanguard fort has been pathetically insecure and somewhat useless.
Hopefully with the adjustments I've made it can see a bit more use in
being more secure; and acting as a strong point in the lowtown.

Drakian Statblock Buff (#2879)

## About The Pull Request

The Drakian statblock is lame and underwhelming, and feels bad to play
with. They have lore about being mighty and bloody conquerors, and yet
the only physical claim to might they have is a +1 to constitution, at
the cost of a -1 to speed, which is a pretty significant hit. I want to
increase their might by giving them a +1 to strength, at the cost of a
-1 to endurance-- they have to lug around those big-ass wings with them
everywhere they go, after all. Probably gets tiring.

## Why It's Good For The Game

Big and strong dragons should be strong. Makes Drakian more of a
competitive race pick for more roles, rather than an active handicap in
most cases. I'd say it does well to even the scales as far as power
goes. Previously, the statblock was just objectively bad. With the new
proposed one, their statblock will be better- and the -1 to endurance
will serve to make sure that it's not TOO much better, in a way that
makes sense, and still embodies their identity as a race.

## Proof of Testing (Required)

Just a numbers change, doesn't really need testing.
